title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 10: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Mia picked 18 apples on Monday and 25 apples on Tuesday. How many apples did she pick in total?
questions.solution: 18 + 25 = 43 apples.

questions.question_text: How many small squares make up the shaded rectangle shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='100' height='40'/><line x1='35' y1='10' x2='35' y2='50'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='50'/><line x1='85' y1='10' x2='85' y2='50'/><line x1='10' y1='30' x2='110' y2='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into a 4 by 2 grid of small squares.
questions.solution: 4 columns times 2 rows equals 8 small squares.

questions.question_text: A movie begins at 3:15 pm and lasts 1 hour and 30 minutes. When does it end?
questions.solution: 3:15 pm plus 1 hour is 4:15 pm, plus 30 minutes is 4:45 pm.

questions.question_text: A notebook costs 4 dollars and a pen costs 2 dollars. How much do 2 notebooks and 3 pens cost together?
questions.solution: 2 x 4 = 8 dollars for notebooks. 3 x 2 = 6 dollars for pens. Total: 8 + 6 = 14 dollars.

questions.question_text: Which number continues the pattern shown: 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, __?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 30' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='1'><text x='5' y='20' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The number sequence 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ? written in a row.
questions.solution: Each term increases by 3, so 14 + 3 = 17.

questions.question_text: Which shape shown has exactly 5 sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='15,45 35,15 55,45'/><rect x='75' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><polygon points='130,10 150,25 145,50 115,50 110,25'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='15'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four shapes in a row: a triangle, a square, a pentagon, and a circle.
questions.solution: A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.question_text: What number is 10 less than 83?
A pencil is 12 centimeters long. How many millimeters long is it?
questions.solution: 1 cm = 10 mm, so 12 cm = 120 mm.

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Sam. Sam is taller than Leo. Leo is taller than Max. Who is the shortest of the four?
questions.solution: Ordering from tallest to shortest: Tom, Sam, Leo, Max. Max is shortest.

questions.question_text: The figure shows a cross made of 5 equal squares. If each square has side 2 cm, what is the total area of the cross?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A plus-sign shape made from 5 equal squares: one center square with one square on each side.
questions.solution: Each square has area 2 x 2 = 4 cm squared. 5 squares give 5 x 4 = 20 cm squared.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Ana eats 2 slices and Ben eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='30'/><line x1='40' y1='10' x2='40' y2='70'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='70' y2='40'/><line x1='18' y1='18' x2='62' y2='62'/><line x1='62' y1='18' x2='18' y2='62'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices by two diameters and two diagonals.
questions.solution: Together they eat 5 slices, leaving 8 - 5 = 3 slices, or 3/8.

questions.question_text: What is the missing number in the equation: 7 x __ = 56?
questions.solution: 56 divided by 7 equals 8.

questions.question_text: In a row of shapes shown, which shape is in the 10th position if the pattern continues?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='35' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='75,10 85,30 65,30'/><circle cx='105' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='125' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='165,10 175,30 155,30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern: circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes. Position 10 = 3 x 3 + 1, so the first shape of the cycle, a circle.

questions.question_text: Ella has a 20-dollar bill. She buys a book for 7 dollars and a sandwich for 5 dollars. How much change does she receive?
questions.solution: Total spent: 7 + 5 = 12 dollars. Change: 20 - 12 = 8 dollars.

questions.question_text: A clock shows 12:15. What time will it be 2 hours and 25 minutes later?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='30'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='40' y2='18'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='55' y2='40'/><text x='37' y='14' font-size='6' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='66' y='43' font-size='6' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='38' y='72' font-size='6' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='12' y='43' font-size='6' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: An analog clock showing 12:15, with the hour hand on 12 and the minute hand on 3.

questions.question_text: In a box there are 4 red balls, 3 blue balls, and 5 green balls. If you take one ball without looking, which color are you most likely to pick?
questions.solution: Green has 5 balls, the greatest amount, so it is most likely.

questions.question_text: Four friends sit in a row: Ava, Ben, Cara, and Dan. Ben is not at an end. Ava is to the left of Ben. Cara is to the right of Dan. Which friend is on the far right?
questions.solution: Ben must be in position 2 or 3. With Ava left of Ben and Cara right of Dan, a valid arrangement is Ava, Ben, Dan, Cara. Cara is on the far right.

questions.question_text: The shaded figure shown is made by joining a square of side 4 cm and a right triangle with legs 4 cm and 3 cm. What is the total area?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='50,10 50,50 80,50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square on the left with a right triangle attached to its right side, sharing the vertical edge.
questions.solution: Square area = 4 x 4 = 16. Triangle area = (4 x 3) / 2 = 6. Total = 16 + 6 = 22 cm squared.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 48. What is the largest of the three numbers?
questions.solution: If the middle number is n, then 3n = 48, so n = 16. The largest is 16 + 1 = 17.

questions.question_text: Look at the sequence of figures shown. If figure 1 has 1 dot, figure 2 has 4 dots, figure 3 has 9 dots, how many dots are in figure 5?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='currentColor'><circle cx='10' cy='25' r='3'/><circle cx='40' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='55' cy='15' r='3'/><circle cx='40' cy='35' r='3'/><circle cx='55' cy='35' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='10' r='3'/><circle cx='105' cy='10' r='3'/><circle cx='120' cy='10' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='25' r='3'/><circle cx='105' cy='25' r='3'/><circle cx='120' cy='25' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='3'/><circle cx='105' cy='40' r='3'/><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three figures showing square arrays of dots: 1x1, 2x2, 3x3.
questions.solution: The pattern is square numbers. Figure 5 has 5 x 5 = 25 dots.

questions.question_text: A rope is 180 cm long. It is cut into pieces of 15 cm each. How many pieces are made?
questions.solution: 180 divided by 15 equals 12 pieces.

questions.question_text: Each symbol stands for a different digit. If star + star = 10 and star + moon = 7, what is the value of moon?
questions.solution: Star + star = 10, so star = 5. Then 5 + moon = 7, so moon = 2.

questions.question_text: How many small cubes are used to build the stack shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A stair-step stack: 3 cubes on the bottom row, 2 cubes on the middle row, 1 cube on the top row.
questions.solution: 3 + 2 + 1 = 6 small cubes.

questions.question_text: A farmer has chickens and cows. Altogether there are 10 heads and 32 legs. How many cows are there?
questions.solution: Let c be cows and k be chickens. c + k = 10 and 4c + 2k = 32. Substituting k = 10 - c: 4c + 20 - 2c = 32, so 2c = 12, c = 6.
